## Goal
Replace reqwest usage in src/providers/openai.rs with asupersync HTTP client.

## Background
OpenAI provider handles:
- Chat completions API
- Function calling (tools)
- SSE streaming with data: [DONE] termination
- 645 lines, 3 unit tests

## Implementation
Same pattern as Anthropic migration:
1. Replace reqwest::Client with crate::http::Client
2. Replace request building with asupersync RequestBuilder
3. Replace SSE streaming with SseStream adapter

1. **SSE Format**: Uses "data: [DONE]" as termination marker

- bd-9sa (HTTP client wrapper)
- bd-pwz (SSE streaming adapter)
- bd-37l (Anthropic migration first - establishes pattern)

## Testing
- 3 existing unit tests must pass
- Integration test with mock server

- src/providers/openai.rs

 {"id":"bd-1wc","title":"Implement CLI E2E integration tests with verbose logging","description":"# Implement CLI E2E integration tests with verbose logging

## Goal
Create end-to-end tests that invoke the pi binary directly and verify behavior,
with detailed logging for debugging failures.

## Background
src/main.rs (1581 lines) and src/cli.rs (332 lines) have minimal testing.
The CLI is the primary user interface and needs comprehensive E2E tests.

## Scope Boundary (Important)
- This bead builds the CLI E2E harness + offline CLI flag coverage.
- Provider-dependent scenarios live in:
  - bd-1ub (print mode + stdin)
  - bd-idw (session lifecycle)
  - bd-1o4 (tool enable/disable + error paths)

## Approach

### Test Harness
```rust
// tests/e2e_cli.rs

use std::process::{Command, Stdio};
use std::time::Instant;

struct CliTestHarness {
    binary_path: PathBuf,
    temp_dir: TempDir,
    env: HashMap<String, String>,
    logs: Vec<LogEntry>,
}

struct LogEntry {
    timestamp: Instant,
    level: LogLevel,
    message: String,
    context: HashMap<String, String>,
}

impl CliTestHarness {
    fn new() -> Self {
        let binary = cargo_bin("pi");
        let temp = TempDir::new().unwrap();
        Self {
            binary_path: binary,
            temp_dir: temp,
            env: HashMap::new(),
            logs: Vec::new(),
        }
    }

    fn log(&mut self, level: LogLevel, msg: &str, ctx: &[(&str, &str)]) {
        self.logs.push(LogEntry {
            timestamp: Instant::now(),
            level,
            message: msg.to_string(),
            context: ctx.iter().map(|(k,v)| (k.to_string(), v.to_string())).collect(),
        });
    }

    fn run(&mut self, args: &[&str]) -> CliResult {
        self.log(Info, "Starting CLI", &[("args", &args.join(" "))]);

        let start = Instant::now();
        let output = Command::new(&self.binary_path)
            .args(args)
            .envs(&self.env)
            .current_dir(self.temp_dir.path())
            .output()
            .expect("Failed to run pi");

        let duration = start.elapsed();
        self.log(Info, "CLI completed", &[
            ("exit_code", &output.status.code().unwrap_or(-1).to_string()),
            ("duration_ms", &duration.as_millis().to_string()),
            ("stdout_len", &output.stdout.len().to_string()),
            ("stderr_len", &output.stderr.len().to_string()),
        ]);

        CliResult {
            exit_code: output.status.code().unwrap_or(-1),
            stdout: String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout).to_string(),
            stderr: String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr).to_string(),
            duration,
        }
    }

    fn dump_logs(&self) -> String {
        // Format all logs for debugging
    }
}
```

## Logging Requirements
- Use TestLogger (bd-3ml) in the harness so failures auto-dump logs.
- Log: command, env (redacted), cwd, duration, stdout/stderr snapshot, and artifact paths.
- Attach logs + outputs as artifacts on failure (text snapshots).

## Determinism Requirements
- Offline-only tests here (help/version/list/config). Any provider-dependent test must live in sub-beads and use VCR playback.
- Ensure environment is fully controlled (PI_CONFIG_PATH, PI_SESSIONS_DIR).

## Test Categories (Offline)

### 1. Basic Invocation Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_version_flag() {
    let mut harness = CliTestHarness::new();
    let result = harness.run(&["--version"]);
    assert_eq!(result.exit_code, 0);
    assert!(result.stdout.contains("pi "));
    assert!(result.duration < Duration::from_millis(100));
}

#[test]
fn test_help_flag() {
    let mut harness = CliTestHarness::new();
    let result = harness.run(&["--help"]);
    assert_eq!(result.exit_code, 0);
    assert!(result.stdout.contains("Usage:"));
}

#[test]
fn test_invalid_flag() {
    let mut harness = CliTestHarness::new();
    let result = harness.run(&["--invalid-flag"]);
    assert_ne!(result.exit_code, 0);
    assert!(result.stderr.contains("error"));
}
```

### 2. Model Listing Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_list_models() {
    let mut harness = CliTestHarness::new();
    let result = harness.run(&["--list-models"]);
    assert_eq!(result.exit_code, 0);
    assert!(result.stdout.contains("claude"));
}

#[test]
fn test_list_models_filter() {
    let mut harness = CliTestHarness::new();
    let result = harness.run(&["--list-models", "opus"]);
    assert_eq!(result.exit_code, 0);
    assert!(result.stdout.contains("opus"));
    assert!(!result.stdout.contains("sonnet"));
}
```

### 3. Subcommand Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_list_packages() {
    let mut harness = CliTestHarness::new();
    let result = harness.run(&["list"]);
    assert_eq!(result.exit_code, 0);
}

#[test]
fn test_config_subcommand() {
    let mut harness = CliTestHarness::new();
    let result = harness.run(&["config"]);
    // Verify output shows config paths
}
```

 {"id":"bd-2jh","title":"Implement error handling path tests","description":"# Implement error handling path tests

## Goal
Test all error conditions that can occur during normal operation, with detailed logging.

## Background
Error paths are currently untested but critical for UX:
- Network failures
- API rate limits
- Authentication failures
- Malformed responses
- File system errors
- Timeout scenarios

## Runtime + Determinism
- Do not introduce new tokio-only tests; use `#[asupersync::test]` or a runtime wrapper.
- All API error cases must use VCR playback (no live network).
- File system errors must use temp dirs and deterministic paths.

## Logging Requirements
- Use TestLogger (bd-3ml).
- Log cassette path, error category, and sanitized error details.
- On failure, dump expected vs actual error variants + any response body fragments.

## Test Categories

### Network Errors
```rust
#[asupersync::test]
async fn test_connection_refused() {
    let vcr = VcrRecorder::new("network_connection_refused");
    let provider = AnthropicProvider::new_with_client(vcr.client());

    let result = provider.stream(&context, &options).await;
    assert!(matches!(result, Err(Error::Network { .. })));
}
```

### API Errors
- 429 rate limit (retry-after)
- 401 auth failure
- 403 forbidden
- 400 bad request
- 500 server error
- 529 overloaded

### Malformed Response Errors
- Invalid JSON body
- Missing required fields
- Unexpected SSE event type
- Truncated stream

### File System Errors
- Read permission denied
- Write permission denied
- Disk full (simulate with tempfs limits if feasible)
- Path too long

### Tool Execution Errors
- bash command not found
- bash timeout
- edit file not found

## Dependencies
- bd-1pf (VCR infrastructure for API error testing)

## Files
- tests/error_handling.rs

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All API error codes covered
- [ ] File system errors covered

- [ ] Error messages are helpful

 {"id":"bd-2lg","title":"Implement compaction module unit tests","description":"# Implement compaction module unit tests

## Goal
Add comprehensive unit tests for src/compaction.rs (903 lines, currently 0 tests).

## Background
Compaction is a critical feature that:
- Summarizes old conversation history to save context tokens
- Identifies optimal cut points in message history
- Tracks file operations (read/write/edit) for summary
- Uses LLM to generate summaries

## Module Analysis (src/compaction.rs)

### Key Types
```rust
struct CompactionContext {
    entries: Vec<SessionEntry>,
    config: CompactionConfig,
    token_estimator: TokenEstimator,
}

struct CutPoint {
    entry_index: usize,
    reason: CutPointReason,
    tokens_before: usize,
    tokens_after: usize,
}

enum CutPointReason {
    TokenLimit,
    UserMessageBoundary,
    ToolResultBoundary,
    NaturalBreak,
}

struct FileSummary {
    path: String,
    operations: Vec<FileOp>,
}
```

### Key Functions
- `estimate_tokens(entry: &SessionEntry) -> usize`
- `find_cut_points(entries: &[SessionEntry], config: &Config) -> Vec<CutPoint>`
- `extract_file_operations(entries: &[SessionEntry]) -> Vec<FileSummary>`
- `prepare_compaction(entries: &[SessionEntry], config: &Config) -> CompactionPlan`
- `generate_summary_prompt(entries: &[SessionEntry], files: &[FileSummary]) -> String`

## Test Categories

### 1. Token Estimation Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_estimate_tokens_simple_text() {
    let entry = user_message("Hello, world!");
    let tokens = estimate_tokens(&entry);
    assert!(tokens > 0 && tokens < 10);
}

#[test]
fn test_estimate_tokens_code_block() {
    let entry = assistant_message("```rust\nfn main() {}\n```");
    let tokens = estimate_tokens(&entry);
    // Code blocks should estimate higher
}

#[test]
fn test_estimate_tokens_tool_result() {
    let entry = tool_result("read", "Large file content...");
    let tokens = estimate_tokens(&entry);
    // Verify reasonable estimate
}
```

### 2. Cut Point Detection Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_find_cut_points_token_limit() {
    let entries = generate_entries(100, 1000);  // 100 entries, ~1000 tokens each
    let config = CompactionConfig { max_tokens: 50000 };
    
    let cuts = find_cut_points(&entries, &config);
    
    // Should find cuts when exceeding limit
    assert!(!cuts.is_empty());
    assert!(cuts[0].tokens_after <= config.max_tokens);
}

#[test]
fn test_cut_point_prefers_user_message_boundary() {
    let entries = vec![
        user_message("Q1"),
        assistant_message("A1"),
        user_message("Q2"),  // Preferred cut point
        assistant_message("A2"),
    ];
    
    let cuts = find_cut_points(&entries, &low_limit_config());
    
    // Should cut at user message boundary
    assert_eq!(cuts[0].reason, CutPointReason::UserMessageBoundary);
}

#[test]
fn test_cut_point_preserves_tool_pairs() {
    let entries = vec![
        user_message("Read file"),
        assistant_with_tool_call("read", { "path": "test.txt" }),
        tool_result("read", "File contents..."),
        assistant_message("The file contains..."),
    ];
    
    let cuts = find_cut_points(&entries, &config);
    
    // Should not cut between tool call and result
    for cut in cuts {
        assert_ne!(cut.entry_index, 2);  // Not after tool call
    }
}
```

### 3. File Operation Extraction Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_extract_file_operations_read() {
    let entries = vec![
        tool_result_with_details("read", json!({"path": "src/main.rs", "lines_read": 100})),
    ];
    
    let files = extract_file_operations(&entries);
    
    assert_eq!(files.len(), 1);
    assert_eq!(files[0].path, "src/main.rs");
    assert_eq!(files[0].operations[0], FileOp::Read { lines: 100 });
}

#[test]
fn test_extract_file_operations_write() {
    let entries = vec![
        tool_result_with_details("write", json!({"path": "new.txt", "bytes_written": 1234})),
    ];
    
    let files = extract_file_operations(&entries);
    
    assert_eq!(files[0].operations[0], FileOp::Write { bytes: 1234 });
}

#[test]
fn test_extract_file_operations_edit() {
    let entries = vec![
        tool_result_with_details("edit", json!({
            "path": "src/lib.rs",
            "old_length": 10,
            "new_length": 15
        })),
    ];
    
    let files = extract_file_operations(&entries);
    
    assert!(matches!(files[0].operations[0], FileOp::Edit { .. }));
}

#[test]
fn test_file_operations_grouped_by_path() {
    let entries = vec![
        tool_result_with_details("read", json!({"path": "a.txt"})),
        tool_result_with_details("edit", json!({"path": "a.txt"})),
        tool_result_with_details("read", json!({"path": "b.txt"})),
    ];
    
    let files = extract_file_operations(&entries);
    
    // Files should be deduplicated
    assert_eq!(files.len(), 2);
    assert_eq!(files.iter().find(|f| f.path == "a.txt").unwrap().operations.len(), 2);
}
```

### 4. Summary Prompt Generation Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_summary_prompt_includes_messages() {
    let entries = vec![
        user_message("Help me with X"),
        assistant_message("I'll help with X by doing Y"),
    ];
    
    let prompt = generate_summary_prompt(&entries, &[]);
    
    assert!(prompt.contains("Help me with X"));
    assert!(prompt.contains("Y"));
}

#[test]
fn test_summary_prompt_includes_file_summary() {
    let files = vec![
        FileSummary {
            path: "src/main.rs".into(),
            operations: vec![FileOp::Read { lines: 100 }, FileOp::Edit { old: 10, new: 15 }],
        },
    ];
    
    let prompt = generate_summary_prompt(&[], &files);
    
    assert!(prompt.contains("src/main.rs"));
    assert!(prompt.contains("read"));
    assert!(prompt.contains("edit"));
}
```

### 5. Compaction Plan Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_prepare_compaction_identifies_entries_to_keep() {
    let entries = generate_entries(50, 1000);
    let config = CompactionConfig {
        max_tokens: 30000,
        keep_recent: 10000,
    };
    
    let plan = prepare_compaction(&entries, &config);
    
    // Should keep recent entries
    assert!(plan.entries_to_keep.len() < entries.len());
    assert!(plan.tokens_after_compaction <= config.max_tokens);
}
```

- view() - Render current state

The update() function is the state machine - it needs thorough testing.

- Log initial state, input message, and key state deltas.
- On failure, dump a compact state diff (selected fields) and the rendered view.

### 3. Slash Command Tests

 {"id":"bd-3d8","title":"Implement /theme slash command","description":"# Implement /theme slash command

## Goal
Add /theme command to list available themes and switch between them.

## Command Syntax
```
/theme              - List available themes
/theme dark         - Switch to dark theme
/theme ocean-dark   - Switch to custom theme
```

## Implementation

### Command Handler
```rust
// src/interactive.rs

fn handle_theme_command(&mut self, args: &str) -> Cmd {
    if args.is_empty() {
        // List themes
        let themes = Theme::discover_themes();
        let current = &self.theme.name;
        
        let mut output = String::from("Available themes:\n");
        for path in themes {
            if let Ok(theme) = Theme::load(&path) {
                let marker = if theme.name == *current { "* " } else { "  " };
                output.push_str(&format!("{}{}\n", marker, theme.name));
            }
        }
        output.push_str("\nUse /theme <name> to switch");
        
        self.status_message = Some(output);
        Cmd::None
    } else {
        // Switch theme
        match Theme::load_by_name(args.trim()) {
            Ok(theme) => {
                self.theme = theme.clone();
                self.styles = create_styles(&theme);
                self.status_message = Some(format!("Switched to theme: {}", theme.name));
                
                // Persist preference
                if let Err(e) = self.save_theme_preference(&theme.name) {
                    tracing::warn!("Failed to save theme preference: {}", e);
                }
                
                Cmd::None
            }
            Err(_) => {
                self.status_message = Some(format!("Theme not found: {}", args.trim()));
                Cmd::None
            }
        }
    }
}
```

### Persist Theme Preference
```rust
fn save_theme_preference(&self, theme_name: &str) -> Result<()> {
    // Update settings.json with theme preference
    let settings_path = Config::settings_path();
    let mut settings = Config::load_from_file(&settings_path)?;
    settings.theme = Some(theme_name.to_string());
    settings.save_to_file(&settings_path)?;
    Ok(())
}
```

### Add to Slash Command Router
```rust
fn handle_slash_command(&mut self, input: &str) -> Cmd {
    let (cmd, args) = parse_slash_command(input);
    
    match cmd {
        "theme" => self.handle_theme_command(args),
        "help" => self.handle_help_command(),
        // ... other commands
    }
}
```

### Update /help Output
```
Commands:
  /help      - Show this help
  /model     - Switch model
  /thinking  - Set thinking level
  /theme     - List/switch themes  <-- NEW
  /clear     - Clear conversation
  /exit      - Exit Pi
```

- Test: /theme lists themes
- Test: /theme <name> switches
- Test: Theme persists across restarts
- Test: Invalid theme shows error
